top_findings.summary: The skill's description reveals it performs autonomous code modifications and commits without continuous user consent. While the primary purpose (building, testing, and publishing an NPM package) justifies actions like running build commands, version updates, and Git operations, the skill goes further. Specifically, it states in sections 5.3 and 5.4 that upon CI failure (build or test errors), it will autonomously "fix the code" or "fix the tests," commit these changes, and push them. This grants the skill broad, unsupervised write access to modify source code and repository history—actions that are excessive and risky for an automated release tool. A release tool should notify the user of failures, not autonomously alter code.
